title: What Richness Looks Like on an Ordinary Day
subtitle: Journal prompts for finding abundance in the everyday and the exceptional equally
excerpt: Five journal prompts for examining what wealth actually looks like in your life as it is now, not the version you will have later. The prompts ask for specific ordinary moments, then specific aspirational ones, and work toward what would need to shift for both to sit alongside each other easily.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 62 reflection theme in MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

content.text: Five journal prompts for finding the specific texture of richness in an ordinary day. The common failure mode with 'abundance mindset' work is that it stays abstract: wealth as a vague destination, richness as something that arrives later. These prompts ask for the specific and the current. Set twenty minutes and write without stopping.
New to journalling as practice? Read
Where in today, or in yesterday, did I feel something I would call richness?
Write the specific moment. Not 'I am grateful for my home' but 'I sat with the second coffee and there was nowhere I had to be for thirty minutes.' Specific, physical, dated.
What does wealth look like to me when I picture it?
Write the image without editing it. Then write whether that image includes the things you already have or excludes them.
What simple pleasures do I currently apologise for, mentally or aloud?
Write the ones you minimise when someone asks about your weekend, or the ones you enjoy privately and never mention. Name three.
What luxury would I add to my ordinary week if money allowed it?
Write one specific thing. Not a category, a specific experience. 'A week in Portugal in September' not 'travel'. 'The good linen' not 'nicer things'.
What would need to shift for both (the simple and the exceptional) to sit in your life without conflict?
Write the internal shift first, then the practical one. The internal shift is usually the one that's actually load-bearing.
Adapted from the Day 62 reflection theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
